question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=other and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL they control, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to return a fabricated node map that routes all pinger traffic exclusively to a subset of real consensus nodes, effectively starving the remaining 30%+ nodes of transaction load and disrupting their liveness probes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user supply HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL with an extremely small value (e.g., '1ns') to cause the ticker in main() to fire at near-infinite frequency, generating a transaction storm that overwhelms a targeted subset of consensus nodes (>=30%) and forces them offline without brute-forcing the full network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a very large integer (e.g., 2147483647) so that submitWithRetry() loops indefinitely against a targeted node, consuming all its gRPC connection slots and causing that node to stop processing legitimate transactions, affecting >=30% of nodes if multiple pinger instances are deployed?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to '0s' or a negative duration, causing toDuration() to fall back to 1s but the validation at line 120 to silently correct it, masking the attacker's intent while still allowing rapid retry bursts that concentrate load on specific nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a whitespace-only value into HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK (e.g., '   ') so that envOr() returns the default 'testnet' after TrimSpace, but a separately injected HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST pointing to an attacker-controlled server is still used, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to build a poisoned node map targeting specific consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to an extremely large value (e.g., '999h') so that each HTTP request to the mirror REST endpoint holds an open connection indefinitely, exhausting the mirror node's connection pool and causing it to stop serving node-list responses to other pinger instances, indirectly causing those pingers to fail to discover nodes and halting transaction submission to >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to 0 and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_BASE_BACKOFF to '0s', causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to make a single non-retried request that, if the mirror node is briefly unavailable, returns an empty network map and causes newClient() to fail, shutting down the pinger and stopping all transaction submissions to all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST endpoint serve a nodesEnvelope JSON response that lists only a subset of real consensus nodes (e.g., 2 out of 7), causing the pinger's SDK client to only ever submit transactions to those 2 nodes, effectively removing >=30% of nodes from receiving any pinger-driven traffic and causing their health checks to fail?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints pointing to attacker-controlled IP addresses for a subset of real node account IDs, causing the SDK client to route transactions to those fake endpoints, starving the real nodes of traffic and causing >=30% of them to be marked unhealthy?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return a response with duplicate node_account_id entries mapping to different service_endpoints, causing the network map to be overwritten with attacker-chosen endpoints for specific nodes, redirecting pinger traffic away from >=30% of real consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to the maximum int64 value (9223372036854775807), causing each transfer transaction to attempt to move an astronomically large amount, which the consensus nodes must validate and reject, generating excessive validation load on targeted nodes and causing >=30% of them to become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a negative value (e.g., '-1'), bypassing the amountTinybar == 0 check at line 111, causing submitWithRetry() to submit a transfer with a negative amount that consensus nodes must process and reject, generating malformed transaction load on specific nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to the hardcoded default key '302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137' (visible in plaintext at line 40), and use it to submit transactions from account 0.0.2 at high frequency, targeting specific consensus nodes and causing >=30% of them to be overwhelmed by transaction validation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a malformed duration string (e.g., 'AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA') to HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL, causing toDuration() to silently fall back to 1s, masking the attacker's intent while the pinger runs at a fixed 1s interval that may be faster than intended, increasing transaction submission rate to targeted nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to a non-existent or invalid account ID that passes the non-empty check at line 108 but fails at AccountIDFromString() in submitWithRetry(), causing every tick to produce an error that is logged but not fatal, while the pinger continues running and generating failed transaction attempts that consume node resources?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST endpoint serve a response with an extremely large number of node entries (e.g., 100,000 entries), causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to allocate a massive network map that exhausts the pinger process's memory, causing it to crash and stop submitting transactions to all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return a response body of unbounded size (e.g., gigabytes of JSON), causing json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode() to consume all available memory in the pinger process, crashing it and halting all transaction submissions to the network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=other and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an HTTP endpoint (not HTTPS), enabling a man-in-the-middle attacker on the network path to intercept and modify the nodesEnvelope response, injecting fake service_endpoints that redirect pinger traffic away from >=30% of real consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that envOr() uses strings.TrimSpace() but does not validate the format of HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID, allowing them to inject a syntactically valid but semantically wrong account ID (e.g., '0.0.0') that causes all transactions to be submitted from an invalid operator, generating rejection load on consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_BASE_BACKOFF to '0s', causing the backoff calculation in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() at line 63 to always produce 0 duration, resulting in a tight retry loop that hammers the mirror REST endpoint with requests at maximum speed, exhausting its connection pool and preventing other components from fetching node lists?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return a nodesEnvelope where all node entries have empty service_endpoints but valid grpc_proxy_endpoint fields, causing the network map to be empty (len(network) == 0 at line 127), triggering the 'no usable service_endpoints found' error and preventing the pinger from ever connecting to any consensus node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ns' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to 100, causing the pinger to submit up to 101 transaction attempts per nanosecond tick, generating a massive burst of gRPC connections to the SDK-selected consensus node, exhausting that node's connection limit and causing it to stop processing transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to a very large value (e.g., '24h') and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to 1, causing backoff() to compute 24h * 2^0 = 24h for the first retry sleep, effectively making the pinger submit only one transaction attempt per interval tick with no meaningful retry, reducing transaction throughput to near zero and causing all nodes to lose pinger-driven liveness signals?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to an empty string after TrimSpace (e.g., '   '), causing cfg.network to be '' after line 100, which falls through to the default case in newClient() and returns an error, crashing the pinger at startup and preventing all transaction submissions to all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST endpoint serve a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing port 0 for all entries, causing the host == '' || ep.Port == 0 check at line 118 to skip all endpoints, resulting in an empty network map and a fatal error that prevents the pinger from starting?
